title: validate data-change on false/undefined/null
manager: The data-change validation path isn't exercised for the three JavaScript values most likely to break it — `false`, `undefined`, and `null` — which are exactly the values where "falsy" logic bugs hide. Add tests that pin the intended behavior for each, so a future refactor can't quietly start dropping legitimate `false` updates because someone wrote a truthiness check where they meant a null check.
